skill.name: paper-context-resolver
skill.description: Optional narrow helper skill for README-first AI repo reproduction. Use only when the README and repository files leave a narrow reproduction-critical gap and the task is to resolve a specific paper detail such as dataset split, preprocessing, evaluation protocol, checkpoint mapping, or runtime assumption from primary paper sources while recording conflicts. Do not use for general paper summary, repo scanning, environment setup, command execution, title-only paper lookup, or replacing README guidance by default.
skill.summary: Resolve narrow paper details that block README-first AI repository reproduction.
skill.author: lllllllama

security_audit.summary: AI review dismissed all three static findings as false positives. The weak cryptography matches are plain description text, and the backtick match is Markdown inline code, not command execution.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern in YAML Text
low_findings.description: Static finding evaluated as FALSE_POSITIVE. agents/openai.yaml line 2 is a short natural-language description and contains no cryptographic algorithm, hashing operation, or security-sensitive code. Confidence: 0.97. Confidence reasoning: The file is declarative metadata, and the cited line contains only descriptive text.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is declarative metadata, and the cited line contains only descriptive text.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern in Skill Description
low_findings.description: Static finding evaluated as FALSE_POSITIVE. SKILL.md line 3 is skill frontmatter describing reproduction use cases and contains no cryptographic code or weak algorithm selection. Confidence: 0.97. Confidence reasoning: The cited line is a prose description, and no cryptographic operation appears there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is a prose description, and no cryptographic operation appears there.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backtick Not Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static finding evaluated as FALSE_POSITIVE. SKILL.md line 46 uses inline Markdown code to name references/paper-assisted-reproduction.md and does not execute Ruby, shell, or any external command. Confidence: 0.96. Confidence reasoning: The cited text is a document reference, and there is no executable script context or command invocation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited text is a document reference, and there is no executable script context or command invocation.

content.value_statement: AI repository reproduction often stalls when README files omit one critical paper detail. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to resolve that specific gap from primary sources.
actual_capabilities: Identifies narrow reproduction questions after README and repository review.
Uses linked paper sources to resolve dataset, split, preprocessing, and evaluation details.
Records conflicts between README guidance and paper statements.
Separates direct evidence from inference in reproduction notes.
Limits scope to reproduction-critical details instead of broad paper summaries.
limitations: Does not execute setup commands or modify repository files.
Does not replace README instructions without documenting conflicts.
Does not provide full paper summaries or novelty analysis.
Requires a concrete reproduction gap before use.

use_cases.title: Clarify Evaluation Splits
use_cases.description: Resolve which dataset split or benchmark protocol should be used when the README is incomplete.

use_cases.title: Document README-Paper Conflicts
use_cases.description: Compare a repository instruction with the paper and record the exact disagreement for reproducibility notes.

use_cases.title: Verify Preprocessing Assumptions
use_cases.description: Check paper details for preprocessing or postprocessing choices that affect reproduced metrics.

prompt_templates.title: Resolve a Missing Split
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one missing dataset detail.
prompt_templates.prompt: The README does not name the evaluation split. Use the linked paper to identify the split and note any conflict.

prompt_templates.title: Check a Preprocessing Detail
prompt_templates.prompt: The repo is unclear about preprocessing. Check the primary paper only for this detail and separate evidence from inference.

prompt_templates.title: Compare README and Paper
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request with possible conflicting guidance.
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare the README instruction with the paper for checkpoint mapping. Record which source says what and preserve README-first policy.

prompt_templates.title: Resolve a Reproduction Blocker
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request with multiple known sources.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the README link, official project page, and primary paper to resolve this evaluation protocol gap. Report conflicts and confidence.

output_examples.input: The README does not say whether validation or test split was used for reported metrics.
output_examples.output: The paper states the reported metric uses the test split. The README is silent, so there is no direct conflict.

output_examples.input: The README and paper appear to name different checkpoint variants.
output_examples.output: README says to use the released base checkpoint. The paper reports the large variant. Treat this as a documented conflict.

output_examples.input: The repository omits the image resize rule used before evaluation.
output_examples.output: The paper gives a resize rule for evaluation preprocessing. Use that rule only for this unresolved reproduction detail.

best_practices: Use this skill only after README and repository inspection leaves one concrete gap.
Prefer primary paper links from the README or official project page.
Record conflicts instead of silently replacing repository instructions.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for broad paper summaries.
Do not use it to search from a title alone without a concrete gap.
Do not infer best settings when no primary source supports them.

faq.question: What problem does this skill solve?
faq.answer: It resolves narrow paper details that block AI repository reproduction.

faq.question: Can it summarize a full paper?
faq.answer: No. It is designed for reproduction-critical details, not general paper explanation.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: It supports Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.

faq.question: Does it override the README?
faq.answer: No. It preserves README-first guidance and documents conflicts when sources disagree.

faq.question: What sources should it prefer?
faq.answer: It should prefer README-linked papers, official project pages, arXiv, and OpenReview records.

faq.question: When should I avoid it?
faq.answer: Avoid it when the README is complete or when the request is only a broad paper summary.
